title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Code Recall
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs human reports, machine output, terminal width, and accessibility from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for accessible human and machine report system after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.
Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

options.text: The Structured Alternative record says accessible human and machine report system looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Structured Alternative.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Structured Alternative for accessible human and machine report system.
The record names the assumption, applies Structured Alternative, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Structured Alternative
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Structured Alternative: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Bookbot team handles case 2019 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Structured Alternative.
steps.why: Retrieving Structured Alternative through the concrete accessible human and machine report system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Structured Alternative, keep Structured Alternative connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Structured Alternative, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Structured Alternative precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Structured Alternative, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 2019 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Structured Alternative, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A visually aligned table is automatically understandable to assistive technology.
